name: Kohler Clarity Water Filter
description: The Kohler Clarity is a tabletop household filter with a silver-impregnated ceramic filter cartridge.

taxonomy.sub_category: Kohler Clarity Water Filter Updated on January 11, 2024 · Created on September 28, 2018 Kohler Clarity Water Filter The Kohler Clarity is a tabletop household filter with a silver-impregnated ceramic filter cartridge. Developed By Kohler Tested By Biological Consulting Services of North Florida Tufts University Content Partners Centre for Affordable Water and Sanitation Technologies (CAWST) Info Tags Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description The Kohler Clarity is a household tabletop water filter with a silver-impregnated ceramic filter cartridge. It holds 11 liters of unfiltered water and 12 liters of filtered water with 99% removal of bacteria and protozoa. Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 3: Good Health and Well-Being Market Suggested Retail Price $65.00 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household Distributors / Implementing Organizations Water Mission, World Vision, Rotary International and Operation Blessing. Competitive Landscape Direct competitors include Rainfresh MP4U Gravity Filter , Tulip Table Top Filter , and Nazava Water Filter . Regions Worldwide Manufacturing/Building Method The Kohler Clarity is manufactured in Arkansas, USA and made out of durable food-safe plastic. The Clarity filter cartridge is made out of silver-impregnated ceramic. Pallets ship with extra spigots and filter cartridges. Intellectural Property Type Trademark User Provision Model Users can obtain the product through distributions by a partner organization or by ordering online from Business Connect. Distributions to Date Status Kohler has distributed 15,000 filters since 2016 through partner organizations Water Mission, World Vision, Rotary International and Operation Blessing in Haiti, Honduras, Peru, Puerto Rico, Louisiana USA, Vietnam, Ethiopia, and Lebanon. Type of filter Ceramic candle (cartridge) filter Primary material of construction Silver-impregnated ceramic Additives None Manufacturer-specified flow rate (L/hr) 2 L/hr Bacteria reduction 5.2 log Virus reduction This filter is not designed to treat viruses. Protozoa reduction log 2 reduction of protozoa and cysts Heavy metals and/or arsenic reduction This filter is not designed to treat chemical contaminants such as lead, arsenic or chlorine. Maximum recommended influent turbidity level (NTU) <0.5 Effluent turbidity levels (NTU) Unknown Maintenance schedule When flow is reduced clean the filter cartridge with sandpaper Manufacturer-specified lifetime volume (L) 5000 liters Safe water storage integration 12 L Design Specifications The Kohler Clarity is designed to filter microbiologically contaminated drinking water through a replaceable ceramic filter for a family of four, producing up to 40 liters of water per day with 12 liters of safe storage. On their webpage, the manufacturer claims that the filter reduces bacteria, protozoa, and cysts by 2 logs, however, testing at Tufts University determined a >5.2 log reduction of E. coli through the filters. This product does not treat chemical contamination. Product Schematics Technical Support Technical support is available from the instruction manual in English, French, and Spanish. Instructions are also available in a series of videos and pictorial instructions are printed on the underside of the plastic lid as pictured below. Replacement Components The Clarity filter cartridge is available online for 15 USD. The Kohler Clarity ceramic filter cartridge is part of the Springboard Initiative’s C1 Common Interface so the replacement filters from other manufacturers will also work with the Kohler Clarity. Lifecycle The filter has the capacity to treat 5,000 liters before it needs replacing. There is no end-of-life indicator so users are recommended to check the filter cartridge periodically for cracks, and to regularly clean it with sandpaper when flow is reduced. When cleaning with sandpaper does not restore flow due to irreversible fouling or there are cracks present in the cartridge, the filter cartridge needs to be replaced. Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters The Kohler Clarity is designed to meet WHO (World Health Organization) water quality standards for microbiological contamination, filter up to 40 liters per day at a rate of 2 liters per hour, have a 23 liters total water capacity (11 L dirty, 12 L clean with safe storage), and be easy to assemble and clean. It is also nestable for shipping as individual filters or in bulk. Vetted Performance Status Biological testing done by Tufts University showed 5 log E. coli removal, and Biological Consulting Services found compliance with the USEPA P231 Protocol (Microbiological Water Purifiers) and additional Kohler-specified protocols for Salmonella, Shigella, parasites, and E. coli. Safety No known safety risks if used properly. Complementary Technical Systems A pre-filter is required for the treatment of turbid water. Academic Research and References Biological testing by Tufts University in 2015 Csrwire.com.
